channel.title: SleepCycler — Sleep Science Blog
channel.description: Evidence-based articles on sleep cycles, bedtime math, sleep deprivation, and how to wake up feeling rested.

item.title: Why Do I Wake Up at 3am Every Night?
item.description: The seven most common causes of middle-of-the-night waking — cortisol, blood sugar, alcohol, cycle boundaries, anxiety, nocturia, sleep apnea — ranked by likelihood, with a 2-week fix protocol.

item.title: How to Fall Asleep Fast — 9 Methods Backed by Science
item.description: From the military method to cognitive shuffling — nine evidence-based techniques ranked by how quickly they produce results.

item.title: What Time Should I Go to Bed If I Wake Up at 5am?
item.description: Exact bedtimes for early risers — why 5am alarms are unforgiving and how cycle alignment changes everything.

item.title: Can You Catch Up on Sleep on the Weekend?
item.description: What the research actually says about weekend recovery sleep, social jet lag, and the limits of catch-up.

item.title: Why Am I Still Tired After 8 Hours of Sleep?
item.description: Six reasons 8 hours leaves you groggy — and why cycle timing matters more than duration.

item.title: What One Night of Bad Sleep Actually Does to Your Body and Brain
item.description: The cognitive, physical, and emotional effects of a single night of poor sleep — backed by research from UPenn, Harvard, and UC Berkeley.

item.title: The Best Time to Wake Up for Deep Sleep (And Why It's Not About Hours)
item.description: Why 6 hours of well-timed sleep can feel better than 8 hours interrupted mid-cycle.

item.title: How Many Sleep Cycles Do You Need? (By Age and Sleep Goal)
item.description: The difference between 4, 5, and 6 cycles — and what happens when you consistently miss one.

item.title: What Time Should I Go to Bed If I Wake Up at 7am?
item.description: Optimal bedtimes for a 7am alarm — from 4.5 hours to a full 9 hours of cycle-aligned sleep.

item.title: What Time Should I Go to Bed If I Wake Up at 6am?
item.description: Exact bedtimes calculated using 90-minute sleep cycles, with the math behind each recommendation.
